meta.title: Privacy policy
meta.description: How Jobbliggaren processes your personal data: purposes, legal basis, retention periods, your rights and security. No tracking and no AI.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: This privacy policy describes how Jobbliggaren processes your personal data, why, on what legal basis and for how long. It also covers your rights and how to exercise them. Jobbliggaren is a free service that helps you find and keep track of your job applications, built on open data about job openings from Arbetsförmedlingen JobTech (Platsbanken).

sections.heading: Controller and contact
paragraphs: The controller for the processing of your personal data in Jobbliggaren is Klas Olsson, a private individual, who runs Jobbliggaren as a non-profit hobby project. You can reach the controller at klasolsson81@gmail.com with any question about this policy and about how your personal data is processed.
It is the controller who determines why and how your data is processed, and who is responsible for ensuring that the processing complies with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

sections.heading: Data protection officer
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren has not appointed a data protection officer. The service is not subject to the requirement for a data protection officer under Article 37 of the General Data Protection Regulation. Instead, you contact us directly using the contact details above for any question about your personal data.

sections.heading: Account, profile and applications (legal basis: contract)
paragraphs: In order for you to create an account and use Jobbliggaren, we process the data below. The legal basis is that the processing is necessary for us to deliver the service to you (Article 6(1)(b), performance of a contract). Without this data, we cannot provide an account.
Your CV, your cover letters, your notes and your follow-ups are more sensitive data and are protected with encryption, see the section on security further down.

A CV file you upload may contain information about other people, for example references. Such information is processed only as part of your own document, for example when the file's text is read out for your own CV review. It is never used for any other purpose, never shown to anyone else and never used to contact anyone. The file is stored encrypted and only for your own use.
list: Email address, password (stored as a cryptographic hash, never in plaintext), display name and account identifier, as well as an identifier from an external login service if you log in via one. Used to create and secure your account and to identify you in the service.

Display name, language preference and your matching preferences, for example occupation, region, type of employment and skills you want to match against. Used to show relevant jobs and to tailor the service to your choices.
Your CV and content parsed from it. Used to store, display and work with your CV in the service.

Your uploaded original CV file. When you import a CV, we store the file you uploaded in its original form and encrypted, so you can download it again and later continue working from it. If the file's text contains a personal identity number, the file is stored only if you explicitly approve it when you upload the file; without your approval it is not stored. You can withdraw your choice at any time by removing the draft or the CV, and the file is then deleted immediately.
Your applications, cover letters, personal notes and follow-ups linked to jobs. Used to help you keep track of your applications and to remind you about follow-ups.

Application history and company overview (planned). We compile your own application history per employer so you can see how many times you have applied to a company, when you last applied and at which stage an application ended. The compilation may be incomplete: an application is only counted when we have the employer's identity from the ad, so the number we show is a minimum and an employer may be missing entirely. The compilation is shown only to you. This is planned and not yet in operation.

sections.heading: Security, operations and product features (legal basis: legitimate interest)
paragraphs: For certain purposes we rely on legitimate interest (Article 6(1)(f)). This means that we have carried out a balancing test and concluded that the processing is necessary for our legitimate interest and does not override your interest in the protection of your data. You have the right to object to processing based on legitimate interest, see the section on your rights.
list: Security and event log. We log events in the service, for example logins, together with the IP address and browser information. Our legitimate interest is to protect the service and your account against misuse, intrusion and incorrect use, and to be able to investigate security events.

Retrieval of job ads from JobTech. We retrieve and process open data about job openings from Arbetsförmedlingen JobTech. Our legitimate interest is to be able to offer a functioning job service. The ads we retrieve are public data and are not your personal data. If an ad contains details of a contact person at the employer, we process those details as described in the section Contact details in job ads below.

Marking what is new since your last visit. We keep track of what is new for you since you were last logged in, so that you can quickly see what has been added. Our legitimate interest is to give you a useful and clear service.

Company lookups against SCB's business register (planned). When you search for an organisation number under Companies, the number you enter is sent to Statistics Sweden (SCB) to fetch the company's name. Our legitimate interest is enabling you to find and follow employers, including those with no ads with us. Numbers that may be personal identity numbers (sole proprietorships) are never looked up and never sent. This is planned and not yet in operation.

sections.heading: Contact details in job ads (legal basis: legitimate interest)
paragraphs: Job ads from Platsbanken can contain details of a contact person at the employer, for example a name, a work email address and a work phone number. Before an ad is stored, we automatically remove email addresses and phone numbers from the ad text. The contact person's details are instead stored in a structured, separate field outside the free-text search, so that you can follow up an application with the right person. The removal from the text is rule-based and can miss details written in unusual ways or embedded in an image.

The contact details are deleted when the ad can no longer be found in the service. If you are a contact person in an ad, more information and your rights are described on the page Are you a contact person in a job ad, linked below under related pages.

sections.heading: Background watch notifications (legal basis: consent)
paragraphs: If you want, you can turn on notifications about new jobs that match your preferences. That processing only takes place if you actively turn it on yourself. The legal basis is your consent (Article 6(1)(a)). The feature is off by default. If you turn it on, we store a record that you gave your consent and when, so that we can demonstrate that the processing is lawful.

You can withdraw your consent at any time by turning off the notifications in your settings. We then stop sending them. Withdrawing your consent does not affect the fact that the processing was lawful before you withdrew it.

sections.heading: Recipients of data
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren never sells your data and does not share it for marketing purposes. In order to operate the service, we engage certain suppliers that process data on our behalf (data processors). We have data processing agreements with them.
Arbetsförmedlingen JobTech (Platsbanken) is our source of open data about job openings, not a recipient of your personal data. We do not send any data about you to JobTech.

Statistics Sweden (SCB) is planned to receive the organisation numbers looked up in the company search: when you search for an organisation number, it is sent to SCB's business register to fetch the company's name. SCB is an independent public authority with its own data-controller responsibility for its register and is not our data processor. This is planned and not yet in operation.

Jobbliggaren currently does not send any email notifications, and no data about you is disclosed to any email provider. If email notifications are introduced in the future, we will update this policy and inform you about which provider is engaged and how your data is protected before the feature is activated.
list: Hetzner (planned hosting provider, EU). Jobbliggaren is planned to be hosted at Hetzner in the EU, where the application and the database run. This is planned and not yet in operation.
Cloudflare (planned DNS and CDN provider, EU). Cloudflare is planned to handle name resolution and traffic up to the service. This is planned and not yet in operation.

sections.heading: Transfer to third countries
paragraphs: We process your data within the EU/EEA. At present, no transfers of your personal data take place to countries outside the EU/EEA. If we were to engage a supplier outside the EU/EEA in the future, we will ensure a legal basis for the transfer under Chapter V of the General Data Protection Regulation, for example the European Commission's standard contractual clauses or a valid adequacy decision, and we will update this policy before any such transfer takes place.

sections.heading: How long we keep data
paragraphs: We do not keep your data longer than necessary for the purposes above.
list: Your account and content (CV, applications, letters, notes, follow-ups) are kept for as long as your account is active, so that you can use the service. You can delete individual content or your entire account at any time.

Your uploaded original CV file. The original file belongs to the CV or draft you imported it into. If you remove the draft or delete the CV, the original file is deleted immediately too, and if you delete your entire account it is removed together with your other data. If you start an import without completing it, the file is cleaned up automatically after at most 90 days.

When you delete your account, the data is removed and the encrypted data is made permanently unreadable, see the section on security. After you have requested deletion, there is a 30-day period during which an account can be restored, before it is deleted permanently.
The security and event log is kept for 90 days and is then cleared automatically. When your account is deleted permanently, the data in the log that can be linked to you is de-identified.
Job ads from JobTech are cleared within 30 days.

Application history (planned). Your applications, including the stage at which an application ended, are kept while your account exists and are removed when the account is deleted per the points above. The link between an application and an employer is not covered by that guarantee: it exists only where we have the employer's identity from the ad. The per-employer compilation can therefore lose entries permanently.

sections.heading: Your rights
paragraphs: Under the General Data Protection Regulation, you have the following rights. You exercise them by contacting us at the email address above. We respond without undue delay and, as a rule, within one month.
list: Access. You have the right to find out whether we process data about you and to obtain a copy of it.
Rectification. You have the right to have inaccurate data corrected and incomplete data completed. Some data, such as profile and CV, you can also change directly in the service.
Erasure. You have the right to have your data erased. You can delete your account in the service, which removes your data.
Restriction. You have the right to request that the processing be restricted in certain cases, for example while an objection or a question about accuracy is being investigated.
Objection. You have the right to object to processing based on legitimate interest. We then stop that processing unless we have compelling legitimate grounds that override your interest.

Data portability. For data you have provided yourself and that is processed on the basis of a contract or consent, you have the right to receive it in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format, and to have it transferred to another party where technically possible.

Withdraw consent. Where the processing is based on your consent, you can withdraw your consent at any time in your settings, without it affecting the lawfulness of the processing carried out beforehand.

sections.heading: Complaint to a supervisory authority
paragraphs: If you consider that we process your personal data incorrectly, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Swedish Authority for Privacy Protection (IMY), which is the supervisory authority for data protection in Sweden. You can find contact details and information at imy.se. We are also happy to receive your feedback directly, so that we can correct any errors.

sections.heading: Requirements for using the service
paragraphs: To create an account you need to provide an email address. It is necessary for us to be able to identify you, secure your account and communicate with you about the service. You cannot create an account without an email address. Providing the data is a requirement for entering into the agreement to use Jobbliggaren, not a statutory requirement. Other data, such as CV and matching preferences, you provide yourself when and if you want to use those features.

sections.heading: No automated decisions
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren makes no automated decisions with legal or similarly significant effect for you. There is no such decision-making under Article 22 of the GDPR.

The matching between you and jobs is rule-based, predictable and explainable. It is based on the preferences and data you provide yourself and on a fixed comparison against the ads. We always show which keywords match and which are missing, so that you can see why a job is suggested. Jobbliggaren uses no AI and no language models for matching, assessment or decisions. It is always you who decides which jobs you apply for.

Jobbliggaren plans an overview of your own application history per employer, for example how many times you have applied to a company, when you last applied and at which stage an application ended. The overview is rule-based and explainable, builds solely on data you created in the service yourself, is shown only to you and is never shared with employers. The number shown is a minimum: an application is only counted when we have the employer's identity from the ad, so the overview may be incomplete. It is not used to make any decisions about you. This is planned and not yet in operation.

sections.heading: Security
paragraphs: We protect your data with technical and organisational measures. Your most sensitive data, that is your CV, your cover letters, your notes and your follow-ups, is encrypted field by field with strong encryption (AES-256-GCM) and with an encryption key that is unique to you.

When you delete your account, your encryption key is destroyed. This makes your encrypted data permanently unreadable and impossible to recreate, even from a possible backup (so-called crypto-erasure). In this way your data truly disappears when you choose to leave the service.

sections.heading: Cookies
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren uses only necessary and functional first-party cookies and no tracking, analytics or marketing cookies. How cookies and browser storage work is described in our cookie policy.

sections.heading: Changes to this policy
paragraphs: We may update this privacy policy, for example if the service develops or if requirements change. The latest version is always available on this page, with the date of the most recent update at the top. For more significant changes, we will inform you in an appropriate way.

meta.title: About Jobbliggaren
meta.description: Jobbliggaren is a free Swedish service for finding jobs and keeping track of your applications, built on Arbetsförmedlingen's open data. Created by Klas Olsson.

about.title: About Jobbliggaren
about.intro: Jobbliggaren is a free Swedish service that helps you find jobs and keep track of your applications in one place. The service is built on open job data from Arbetsförmedlingen JobTech (Platsbanken), so that you can search for, save and follow up on your applications at no cost.
whyParagraphs: Jobbliggaren is built by Klas Olsson, a software developer focused on .NET and AI, based in Göteborg. It is an independent hobby project, not a company.

The motivation is the same as behind his earlier app Kalaskoll, which helps parents plan children's parties: he was missing a simple and complete solution to a concrete everyday problem, so he built one himself. For Jobbliggaren, the problem was keeping track of your job search. There was no single, free app that both helped you find jobs that match and kept track of which jobs you had applied for and how it went. Jobbliggaren is the attempt to solve that.
about.valuesIntro: Jobbliggaren is built as a public-benefit service, in the same spirit as public e-services: simple, factual and reliable.
values: Free. The service is free to use.
Built on open data. The jobs come from Arbetsförmedlingen's open JobTech data.

Explainable matching, no AI. The matching between you and the jobs is rule-based and always shows which requirements and keywords match and which are missing. Jobbliggaren uses no AI and makes no automated decisions about you.
Your privacy first. Your most sensitive data, such as your CV, is encrypted, and you can delete your account and your data whenever you want. We use no tracking.
about.moreHeading: More about Klas and his projects
about.moreText: You can read more about Klas and his other projects on his website, and see Kalaskoll, his app for children's parties.
about.contactText: If you have questions, ideas or find something that does not work, feel free to get in touch.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.intro: Jobbliggaren is a small hobby project, and we are glad to receive questions, ideas and bug reports. Get in touch and we will reply as soon as we can.
contact.emailLabel: You can reach us by email:
contact.aboutListIntro: Feel free to get in touch about:
aboutList: questions about how the service works,
bugs and errors you come across,
questions about your personal data or your privacy.

meta.title: Terms of use
meta.description: Terms of use for Jobbliggaren, a free service for finding jobs and keeping track of your applications. Built on Arbetsförmedlingen's open data.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.intro: These terms of use apply to your use of Jobbliggaren. Jobbliggaren is a free service that helps you find and keep track of your job applications, built on open job data from Arbetsförmedlingen JobTech (Platsbanken). By creating an account and using the service, you accept the terms below. How we process your personal data is described in the privacy policy, and which cookies we use is described in the cookie policy.

sections.heading: Who provides the service
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren is provided by Klas Olsson as a non-profit hobby project and is not run commercially. You can reach us by email at klasolsson81@gmail.com, where you can also send questions about the terms.

sections.heading: What Jobbliggaren is
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren is a tool for finding jobs and gathering your applications in one place. The service retrieves public job ads from Arbetsförmedlingen's open JobTech data (Platsbanken) and lets you search and filter ads, save ads, add your CV and your application texts, keep notes and follow-ups, and get a match between your data and the ads.

The matching is rule-based and predictable. It shows which keywords and requirements match and which are missing, so that you can see for yourself why a job matches. Jobbliggaren uses no AI and makes no automated decisions with legal or similarly significant effect for you. The matching is a support, not a verdict on your chances of getting a job.

Jobbliggaren is free. We do not charge you for creating an account or using the service. Because the service is free and under development, features may be added, changed or removed. We strive to keep the service available, but we cannot guarantee that it is always free of interruptions or errors.

sections.heading: Who can use the service
paragraphs: To create an account you need a valid email address. You are responsible for ensuring that the data you provide is accurate and for keeping your login details protected. If you suspect that someone unauthorised has gained access to your account, you should change your password and contact us.
The service is aimed at you who are looking for work and is not intended for children. If you are under 18, you should have a guardian's consent before using the service.

sections.heading: Your account and how the service may be used
paragraphs: Your account is personal. You are responsible for the content you upload, for example your CV, your application texts, notes and follow-ups. You are also responsible for having the right to use the content you add.
When you use Jobbliggaren, you may not:
list: use the service for anything illegal or to infringe anyone else's rights,
upload content that is illegal, offensive or that is not yours to share,
try to access other users' accounts or data,
disrupt, overload or try to circumvent the security of the service, or automatically harvest data from the service in a way that loads it unreasonably,
use the service in a way that harms Jobbliggaren or other users.

sections.heading: Who owns what
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren as a service, including the software, the source code, the interface, the name and the trademark Jobbliggaren, belongs to Klas Olsson or its licensors. Your use of the service does not give you any right to this beyond what is needed to use Jobbliggaren in the ordinary way.

The content you upload yourself, for example your CV and your application texts, is and remains yours. You retain all rights to it. In order to run the service for you, you grant us a limited right to process the content for that purpose, for example to store, display and match it against ads. That right applies only for as long as it is needed to deliver the service to you and ends when you delete the content or your account. We do not use your content for any other purpose, and we do not sell it on.

The job ads come from Arbetsförmedlingen's open data via JobTech (Platsbanken). That data belongs to its respective sources and is provided as open data by Arbetsförmedlingen. Jobbliggaren is not responsible for the content of individual ads and cannot guarantee that an ad is current, correct or still valid. It is the employer or whoever published the ad who is responsible for its content.

sections.heading: The service is provided as is
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren is provided free of charge and as is. We do our best to keep the service useful and correct, but we cannot guarantee that job ads are always current, that the matching suits every situation or that the service is always available without interruption.
We are not responsible for decisions you make based on the information in the service, for example which jobs you apply for, nor for the content of the job ads retrieved from open data.

We limit our liability as far as the law allows. We do not remove or restrict any liability that under mandatory law may not be limited. If you are a consumer, your rights under mandatory consumer protection legislation always apply, and a term that would be unfair to you does not apply in that part.

sections.heading: Changes to the terms
paragraphs: We may update these terms, for example if the service develops or if requirements change. The latest version is always available on this page, with the date of the most recent update at the top. For more significant changes, we try to inform you in reasonable time before they take effect. If you continue to use Jobbliggaren after a change has taken effect, you accept the updated version. If you do not want to accept the change, you can close your account.

sections.heading: Closing and deleting the account
paragraphs: You can stop using Jobbliggaren and delete your account at any time. When you delete the account, your data is removed as described in the privacy policy. Your encrypted content is made unreadable by discarding your encryption key, and your data is deleted in the service. Some data may remain for a short time or to the extent required by law.

We may need to close or restrict an account if it is used in breach of these terms or if we are required to do so by law. If we were to need to discontinue the service, we will inform you in reasonable time in advance, so that you have time to save the content you want to keep.

sections.heading: Applicable law and disputes
paragraphs: Swedish law applies to these terms and to your use of Jobbliggaren. Disputes are heard by the Swedish general courts, unless otherwise follows from mandatory law.

If you are a consumer and not satisfied, we would like you to contact us first, so that we can try to resolve the matter. You can also turn to the Swedish National Board for Consumer Disputes (ARN), arn.se, if the matter meets their requirements to be heard, or use the European Commission's platform for online dispute resolution (ODR), ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr.

meta.title: Cookie policy
meta.description: Which cookies Jobbliggaren uses and why. Only necessary and functional cookies, no tracking and no consent banner.

cookies.title: Cookie policy
cookies.intro: This cookie policy describes which cookies and similar storage techniques Jobbliggaren uses, why they are used and how you control them. The policy is informational. Jobbliggaren uses no tracking, analytics or marketing cookies, and therefore no consent banner is shown.

sections.heading: What a cookie is
paragraphs: A cookie is a small text file that a website saves in your browser. The file can be read back on a later visit and helps the website, for example, keep you logged in or remember your language choice. Some cookies remain only for as long as your visit lasts (session cookies), others are saved for a set period (persistent cookies).

In addition to cookies, Jobbliggaren also uses storage directly in your browser (so-called localStorage) for a couple of features. That storage is never sent to our servers and is described separately further down.

sections.heading: Necessary cookies
paragraphs: Necessary cookies are required for basic functions to work, above all login. Without them you cannot log in or use your account. These cookies are not set for visitors who do not log in.

When you log in you can choose Keep me logged in. If you leave the box unticked, a session cookie is set that disappears when you close the browser, which is the safe default on shared computers. If you tick the box, a persistent cookie is set so that you stay logged in on this device for up to 180 days. For security the session id is automatically rotated at regular intervals without logging you out. You can log out at any time with Log out, which is available on every signed-in page and removes the cookie immediately.
list: __Host-jobbliggaren_session: keeps you logged in. It contains only a random session id, no personal data. It is set with HttpOnly, Secure and SameSite=Strict. If you chose Keep me logged in it lasts up to 180 days, otherwise it disappears when you close the browser.
__Host-jobbliggaren_refresh_after: a technical helper cookie with no personal data that governs how often your login is refreshed in the background. It contains only a timestamp.

Protection against forged requests (CSRF): Jobbliggaren protects you against so-called cross-site request forgery attacks. The protection builds on how the session cookie is configured (SameSite=Strict) together with a server-side check of where the request comes from. No special, separate protection cookie is required.

sections.heading: Functional cookies
paragraphs: Functional cookies remember choices and settings that you make yourself, so that the interface behaves as you expect on your next visit. They are not necessary for the service to work, but they improve your experience. All of them are set with Secure and HttpOnly.
list: NEXT_LOCALE: remembers whether you chose Swedish or English. It is set when you change the language yourself and lasts 365 days.
__Host-jobbliggaren_guest_welcomed and __Host-jobbliggaren_setup_welcomed: remember that you have already closed a welcome box, so that it is not shown again in the same browser. They last 365 days.

sections.heading: Storage in the browser (localStorage)
paragraphs: In addition to cookies, Jobbliggaren saves a couple of simple settings directly in your browser using localStorage. This information never leaves your device and is not sent to our servers. You can clear localStorage at any time via your browser's settings, which does not affect your account or your saved data.
list: Which information boxes you have closed on the overview page.
Your choice of theme (light or dark).

sections.heading: No tracking, analytics or marketing cookies
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren uses no cookies for analytics, statistics, tracking, A/B testing, advertising or marketing. We use no third-party cookies and no tools such as Google Analytics, the Meta pixel or the like. There is no profiling and no automated decisions linked to the cookies.

Because we use only necessary and functional cookies, and the functional ones are set based on choices you make yourself, no consent banner is required under the Electronic Communications Act (2022:482). Instead, we inform you about the cookies here.

sections.heading: How you control cookies
paragraphs: You can control and delete cookies via the settings in your browser. There you can block cookies, delete existing cookies and get a message before a cookie is saved. How you do this differs between browsers.

Keep in mind that if you block or delete the necessary cookies, you cannot log in or use your account. If you block the functional cookies, the service still works, but your choices, for example language, may not be remembered between visits.

sections.heading: If we introduce more cookies in the future
paragraphs: If Jobbliggaren were to introduce in the future any cookie that is not necessary or purely functional, for example for analytics, statistics, A/B testing or marketing, we will first obtain your consent. We will then introduce a solution where it is just as easy to decline as to accept, and where you can withdraw your consent at any time. Such a cookie is not set before you have given your consent, and this policy is updated before the change takes effect.

sections.heading: Changes to this policy
paragraphs: We may update this cookie policy, for example if the service develops or if requirements change. The latest version is always available on this page, with the date of the most recent update at the top.

meta.title: Accessibility statement
meta.description: Accessibility statement for Jobbliggaren: our aim to meet WCAG 2.1 AA, what we do, known gaps and how to report accessibility problems.

accessibility.title: Accessibility statement
accessibility.intro: Jobbliggaren should be usable by as many people as possible, regardless of ability and regardless of the assistive technology you use. We aim to meet the international standard WCAG 2.1 at level AA, the same level that the Swedish public sector follows. Jobbliggaren is a private hobby project and is not covered by the Swedish law on accessibility to digital public service (2018:1937), but we hold ourselves to the same standard voluntarily, because accessibility is part of the service being a public good.

sections.heading: How accessible the service is
paragraphs: We assess that Jobbliggaren partially meets WCAG 2.1 at level AA. Most of the service can be used with the keyboard alone and with a screen reader, but there are known gaps that we are working to fix. They are described further down this page.

sections.heading: What we do for accessibility
paragraphs: Accessibility is built into how Jobbliggaren is structured. In practice that includes the following.
list: You can reach and use everything with the keyboard alone, and it is always clearly visible where focus is.
Every page has a skip-to-content link and clear landmarks, so that you who use a screen reader can quickly skip past menus to the main content.
We use semantic markup with headings, buttons, links and form fields, so that assistive technology understands what each part is. Buttons and icons without visible text have a descriptive label.
We aim for good contrast between text and background, with black or dark text on a light surface for body text.
Status is always conveyed by both colour and text or symbol, never by colour alone.
Pages can be magnified to 200 percent without content disappearing or requiring horizontal scrolling, and the text reflows to the width of the screen.
If you have chosen to limit motion in your operating system, we turn off animations.
The language is plain and concrete, and the matching against jobs is rule-based and explainable. We always show which keywords match and which are missing, and we use no AI.

sections.heading: Known gaps
paragraphs: We are aware that the service is not yet fully accessible. We know about the following gaps and are working on them.
list: The assessment is a self-assessment. The service has not yet been reviewed by an independent party.
Jobbliggaren is developed continuously, and a new feature may temporarily have accessibility gaps until it has been reviewed.
A few minor interface details, for example some status markers, do not yet reach full contrast at level AA. We are reviewing them.
A dark theme is prepared but not yet activated, and has therefore not been assessed for accessibility.

sections.heading: If you run into a barrier
paragraphs: Get in touch if you run into something in Jobbliggaren that you cannot use because of an accessibility gap. Please describe which page it concerns and what does not work, and we will try to fix it. If you need content or a function another way in the meantime, we will help you as far as we can.
You can reach us by email: klasolsson81@gmail.com. We reply without undue delay.

sections.heading: How we have assessed the service
paragraphs: The assessment of how well Jobbliggaren meets WCAG 2.1 AA is based on our own review. We use automated checks with the tools axe and Lighthouse together with manual tests using the keyboard alone and a screen reader. The assessment was last made on 30 June 2026.

meta.title: Help centre
meta.description: Help centre for Jobbliggaren. Here we gather guides and answers about how the service works, how matching and CV review work and how to reach us.

help.title: Help centre
help.intro: Here we gather the help for Jobbliggaren in one place. Below you will find guides and answers about how the service works, how matching and CV review work and how to contact us if you need more help.
help.guidesIntro: Start here if you are wondering how something works or want to get going.
help.moreIntro: If you cannot find the answer, or you run into a barrier, please get in touch.

faq.label: Frequently asked questions

meta.title: For developers
meta.description: Jobbliggaren is a source-available civic project. Here is the code on GitHub, how the service is built, the open job data it rests on and why matching is rule-based with no AI.

developers.title: For developers
developers.intro: This page is for you who are curious about how Jobbliggaren works under the hood. Jobbliggaren is an independent hobby project built in the open, and we are happy to explain how it is made, what data it rests on and why matching looks the way it does.

sections.heading: Source-available code
paragraphs: The source code for Jobbliggaren is public and can be read on GitHub. The project is source-available under PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0, a licence that lets you read, use and build on the code for non-commercial purposes.

It is not a permissive open source licence in the classic sense. Commercial use requires separate permission. The intent is for the code to be open to inspect and learn from, while the service stays a non-profit project.

sections.heading: How Jobbliggaren is built
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren is built with a few well-established tools and a clear architecture, so that the code is easy to follow.
list: Backend in C# and .NET, structured according to Clean Architecture with domain-driven design and CQRS.
Frontend in TypeScript and React with Next.js.
PostgreSQL as the database.
Matching is a rule-based engine. Jobbliggaren uses no AI and no language models, neither for matching, review nor decisions.

sections.heading: Open job data
paragraphs: The jobs in Jobbliggaren come from Arbetsförmedlingen's open data via JobTech (Platsbanken). These are public listings, not personal data about you.
If you want to work with the same raw data yourself, you fetch it directly from Arbetsförmedlingen's JobTech, which provides it as open data for developers.

sections.heading: No public API in this version
paragraphs: Jobbliggaren does not yet offer a public API to build integrations against. The service's own interface is for signed-in users. If you need job data to build on, Arbetsförmedlingen's JobTech is the right source.
Should a public API become relevant later, we will describe it here.

developers.repoText: You will find the source code and the licence in the repository.

meta.title: Are you a contact person in a job ad?
meta.description: Information for contact persons named in job ads: what Jobbliggaren processes, where the data comes from, how long it is kept and your rights.

recruiterNotice.title: Are you a contact person in a job ad?
recruiterNotice.intro: Jobbliggaren retrieves job ads as open data from Arbetsförmedlingen's Platsbanken. If you are named as a contact person in an ad, we therefore process some data about you even though you are not a user of the service. This page describes what we process, why, for how long and which rights you have.

sections.heading: What we process and where the data comes from
paragraphs: We retrieve ads from Arbetsförmedlingen's open JobTech API. If the ad names a contact person, we process the name, role, work email address and work phone number as published in the ad. The data comes from the ad at Arbetsförmedlingen, not from you.

Before the ad text is stored, we automatically remove email addresses and phone numbers from the running text. The contact details are instead stored in a structured, separate field outside the free-text search. The removal is rule-based and can miss details written in unusual ways or embedded in an image.

sections.heading: Why we process the data (legal basis)
paragraphs: The legal basis is legitimate interest (Article 6(1)(f)). The interest is the user's: a jobseeker needs to be able to follow up their own application with the right person at the employer. Arbetsförmedlingen's advertising terms require the advertiser to have the contact person's consent to the publication, and state that ad data may be shown by other actors retrieving ads via the API.
We never use your contact details for marketing, profiling or mailings of our own, and we never sell them.

sections.heading: How long the data is kept
paragraphs: The contact details on an ad are deleted when the ad can no longer be found in the service, for example when it has been archived or has left the Platsbanken feed.
If a user has registered an application for the ad, the ad data is frozen in that user's own application history. The frozen copy's contact details are deleted when the application is closed.

sections.heading: Your rights
paragraphs: You have the right to object to the processing (Article 21). We do not attempt to balance our interest against yours; we honour the objection in full.

You also have the right to request erasure (Article 17). Because your name can appear in the ad's running text, we then erase our entire copy of the ad and block it from being retrieved again. We cannot remove the ad at Arbetsförmedlingen, which is the publisher.

You can reach the data controller at klasolsson81@gmail.com. We respond without undue delay and normally within one month. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Swedish Authority for Privacy Protection (imy.se).
